task.title: Synchronizing manually
task.shortdesc: Follow these steps to manually synchronize a local project or file with a remote system.

context.note: The remote synchronization function is deprecated.

taskbody.result:
checks for changes in the selected resources and displays the Synchronize perspective. The Synchronize perspective displays the resources in which changes occurred and indicates the direction in which the change must be synchronized.
In the Synchronize perspective, there are four possible states, as shown in the following table. You can compare resource content between the local workspace copy and the remote copy, or simply push the change out to the appropriate location. Possible states for resources in which changes occurred
Symbol Meaning Possible actions
Gray arrow that points right An outbound change. This outbound change is a change that occurred only in the local workspace.
Copy the local file to the host by using the Copy changes to host action.
Replace the local file by using the Override changes to local action.
Blue arrow that points left An incoming change. An incoming change indicates that a change occurred only on the host.
Copy the remote resource to the local project by using the Copy changes to local action.
Replace the remote resource by using the Override changes on host action.
Red arrow that points left and right
Conflicting changes. Conflicting changes indicate that changes were made both to the remote version and the local copy. The conflict must be resolved to continue. You can resolve the conflict by taking the following actions:
Indicating that you inspected the conflicts (usually by using the compare editor) and resolved any differences by using the Mark as Merged action.
Replacing the local copy with remote contents by using the Override changes to local action.
Replacing the remote copy with the local contents by using the Override changes on host action.
Red arrow that points left and right with a plus sign An item that is newly mapped and never inspected before. This item is considered a conflict and must be resolved as a conflicted resource.
